NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED (TRANSACTION ID # 57645002) FILED BY DEFENDANT ENNOV SOLUTIONS, INC., A LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNER SHIP

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ENNOV SOLUTIONS, INC.'S, MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS MOOT AS TO PARS. 14-19 [IIED] IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER. THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PAR. (B) OF THE PRAYER [GENERAL/SPECIAL DAMAGES] AND PAR. D [PUNITIVE DAMAGES]. PLAINTIFF CANNOT OBTAIN THOSE DAMAGES BECAUSE THEY ARE ONLY RECOVERABLE ON THE IIED CAUSE OF ACTION. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO PAR. (C) OF THE PRAYER [FRONT AND BACK PAY]. PLAINTIFF CAN RECOVER BACK PAY BASED ON THE OVERTIME. THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PAR. (F) OF THE PRAYER [LIQUIDATED DAMAGES PER LABOR CODE SEC. 1194.2] AS UNOPPOSED AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT HE WAS NOT PAID THE MINIMUM WAGE. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO PAR. (G) OF THE PRAYER [STATUTORY/CIVIL PENALTIES]. LABOR CODE SECS. 203 AND 226 SUPPORT THE PRAYER. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO PAR. (H) OF THE PRAYER [INJUNCTION]. PLAINTIFF CAN RECOVER THAT RELIEF UNDER THE UCL AND LABOR CODE SEC.226(H). THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO PAR. (J) OF THE PRAYER [ATTORNEY'S FEES]. LABOR CODE SEC. 218.5 SUPPORTS THE AWARD. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT

Off Calendar Sep-04-2015 Set For Trial On Apr-11-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-20-2015 Continued To Sep-23-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jesse Jong, of Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices LLP, Tel. 415.626.7744, defendants;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. DEFENDANT ENNOV SOLUTIONS, INC.'S, DEMURRER to Complaint as to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. See CCP 430.10(a). The worker's compensation exclusivity provisions bar the cause of action. The court can address the issue on demurrer. In Gibbs v. American Airlines, Inc. (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 1, 13, the court explains:

Singleton affirmed judgment following an employer's demurrer where the plaintiff alleged injury to an employee within the course of employment but made no mention of workers' compensation insurance. [citation] Importantly, the Court of Appeal found it proper to presume that the employer complied with the law requiring its purchase of workers' compensation insurance. [citation] A plaintiff's action against an employer upon allegations of a work-related injury is barred unless the plaintiff also alleges that the employer has failed to secure the payment of workers' compensation through mandated insurance.

Here, pars. 10-12 of the complaint allege the employment relationship and there is no allegation that defendant lacked the requisite worker's compensation insurance. The injury occurred during plaintiff's employment. (Pars. 10-12 of the Amended Complaint). The court further notes that plaintiff fails to state a cause of action because the failure to pay wages is not outrageous conduct as a matter of law. The court notes that defendant did not raise CCP 430.10(e) [failure to state a cause of action] or argue the outrageousness issue in its moving papers. Both parties, however, briefed the issue in the opposition and reply briefs. Plaintiff cites Cramer v. Consolidated Freightways, Inc. 255 F.3d 683, 697 (9th Cir. 2001) for the proposition that defendant's "criminal" conduct is outrageous per se. Cramer is distinguishable. There, the defendant concealed cameras in restrooms and violated Penal Code sec. 653n. In this simple wage dispute, plaintiff is merely alleging civil violations of the Labor Code. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jesse Jong, of Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices LLP, Tel. 415.626.7744, defendants;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ENNOV SOLUTIONS, INC.'S, MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied as moot as to pars. 14-19 [IIED] in light of the ruling on the demurrer. The motion is granted without leave to amend as to Par. (b) of the prayer [general/special damages] and Par. d [punitive damages]. Plaintiff cannot obtain those damages because they are only recoverable on the IIED cause of action. The motion is denied as to Par. (c) of the prayer [front and back pay]. Plaintiff can recover back pay based on the overtime. The motion is granted without leave to amend as to Par. (f) of the prayer [liquidated damages per Labor Code sec. 1194.2] as unopposed and plaintiff does not allege that he was not paid the minimum wage. The motion is denied as to Par. (g) of the prayer [statutory/civil penalties]. Labor Code secs. 203 and 226 support the prayer. The motion is denied as to Par. (h) of the prayer [injunction]. Plaintiff can recover that relief under the UCL and Labor Code sec.226(h). The motion is denied as to Par. (j) of the prayer [attorney's fees]. Labor Code sec. 218.5 supports the award. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
